{"id":"bd-sov","title":"Remove legacy Beads Classic storage (Wasteland w-gt-001)","description":"Large cleanup: remove all pre-Dolt Beads Classic storage layers from the beads codebase.

This is the beads-side work for Wasteland wanted item w-gt-001 ('Remove legacy Beads Classic code paths').

## Approach
1. FIRST: Identify and document the happy path (Dolt-based storage) — enumerate all code paths that are the current correct way
2. Ensure the happy path has good test coverage and documentation
3. Then systematically remove all legacy storage mechanisms:
   - SQLite storage layer
   - JSONL sync engine
   - Daemon merge code
   - 3-way merge engine remnants
   - Old sync branch exports (CommitToSyncBranch, PullFromSyncBranch, etc.)
   - Any 'classic' or 'legacy' mode switches
4. Review and update CLI entry points — help text, flags, subcommands that reference removed features
5. Ensure all remaining tests pass and add new tests where coverage gaps exist
6. Update documentation to reflect the clean Dolt-only architecture

## Supervision
The crew member should plan this carefully before executing. Break into phases:
- Phase 1: Audit & document (identify all legacy code paths, map the happy path)
- Phase 2: Add/verify tests for the happy path
- Phase 3: Remove legacy code in safe increments
- Phase 4: Clean up CLI, help text, docs
- Phase 5: Final test run and verification","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jason@optionzero.co","created_at":"2026-03-04T14:52:49Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-04T14:52:49Z"}

new_value:
{"notes":"Audit complete. SQLite storage implementation already removed. Remaining work:
1. Clean stale SQLite/JSONL comments in ~60 files
2. Remove migration validation code (migration_validation.go, migration_validation_nocgo.go) - validates pre/post SQLite-to-Dolt migration
3. Remove SQLite artifact scanning (artifacts.go scanSQLiteArtifacts)
4. Fix example extension (examples/bd-example-extension-go) - uses sqlite3 directly
5. Update sql.go help text (says 'SQLite or Dolt')
6. Update context.go comment (says 'SQLite lock timeout')
7. Update doctor.go help text (references JSONL/SQLite)
8. Update history.go help text (references SQLite)
9. Clean deep.go 'SQLite backend detected' message
10. Clean test variable names (sqliteStore in epic_test.go)
11. Remove IsDoltBackend checks where they guard dead SQLite paths
12. Clean doctor/dolt.go N/A SQLite backend message
13. Remove gate.go legacy SQLite comment
14. Clean init_test.go --backend=sqlite deprecation test (KEEP - tests user-facing error msg)
15. Clean configfile_test.go stale sqlite value test (KEEP - tests config validation)"}

old_value:
{"id":"bd-b5l","title":"Stale daemon files cause bd to hang after unclean shutdown","description":"When the beads daemon is killed uncleanly (power outage, SIGKILL, system crash), it leaves behind stale daemon.pid, daemon.lock, and bd.sock files in .beads/. On next bd invocation, bd tries to connect to the dead socket, hangs until timeout, and exits non-zero. The error is opaque — stderr only shows unrelated warnings (e.g. beads.role not configured) while the real failure is the dead daemon connection.

Repro: kill -9 the bd daemon, then run any bd command in that repo.

Fix: On daemon connect failure, bd should check if the PID in daemon.pid is still alive (kill -0). If the process is dead, automatically clean up the stale files (daemon.pid, daemon.lock, bd.sock) and either restart the daemon or fall back to direct database access. This is standard stale-lock recovery.

Files likely involved: wherever daemon connection/startup logic lives — look for references to bd.sock, daemon.pid, daemon.lock.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jason@optionzero.co","created_at":"2026-03-12T16:49:47Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-12T16:49:47Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"attached_molecule: bd-wisp-pckt
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-12T16:50:53Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

When the beads daemon is killed uncleanly (power outage, SIGKILL, system crash), it leaves behind stale daemon.pid, daemon.lock, and bd.sock files in .beads/. On next bd invocation, bd tries to connect to the dead socket, hangs until timeout, and exits non-zero. The error is opaque — stderr only shows unrelated warnings (e.g. beads.role not configured) while the real failure is the dead daemon connection.

Repro: kill -9 the bd daemon, then run any bd command in that repo.

Fix: On daemon connect failure, bd should check if the PID in daemon.pid is still alive (kill -0). If the process is dead, automatically clean up the stale files (daemon.pid, daemon.lock, bd.sock) and either restart the daemon or fall back to direct database access. This is standard stale-lock recovery.

Files likely involved: wherever daemon connection/startup logic lives — look for references to bd.sock, daemon.pid, daemon.lock."}
old_value:
{"id":"bd-b5l","title":"Stale daemon files cause bd to hang after unclean shutdown","description":"When the beads daemon is killed uncleanly (power outage, SIGKILL, system crash), it leaves behind stale daemon.pid, daemon.lock, and bd.sock files in .beads/. On next bd invocation, bd tries to connect to the dead socket, hangs until timeout, and exits non-zero. The error is opaque — stderr only shows unrelated warnings (e.g. beads.role not configured) while the real failure is the dead daemon connection.

Repro: kill -9 the bd daemon, then run any bd command in that repo.

Fix: On daemon connect failure, bd should check if the PID in daemon.pid is still alive (kill -0). If the process is dead, automatically clean up the stale files (daemon.pid, daemon.lock, bd.sock) and either restart the daemon or fall back to direct database access. This is standard stale-lock recovery.

Files likely involved: wherever daemon connection/startup logic lives — look for references to bd.sock, daemon.pid, daemon.lock.","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"beads/polecats/obsidian","owner":"jason@optionzero.co","created_at":"2026-03-12T16:49:47Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-12T16:50:53Z"}
